posts.title: gmtoolkit: a single binary instead of a .NET runtime
posts.excerpt: All my GameMaker ports dropped UndertaleModTool + .NET + the gmKtool Python script for gmtoolkit, a single native C++ binary. UTMT loaded data files into RAM wholesale and would OOM during patching on 1GB devices that ran the game itself just fine.

posts.title: Dusklight v1.2.0 and the OpenGL ES question
posts.excerpt: v1.2.0's headline feature is experimental OpenGL ES, but the path is scoped to Android by design and the Linux branch still hardcodes it off.

posts.title: Game engines as shared runtimes
posts.excerpt: 47 GameMaker and 6 RPG Maker ports moved off per-port bundled engines onto shared squashfs runtimes. A few hundred MB of duplicate binaries that aren't being redownloaded any more, plus why I'm hosting Reborn and Rejuvenation ports and how they deviate from PortMaster's versions.

posts.title: Dusklight: porting Twilight Princess to ARM64 handhelds
posts.excerpt: An unofficial Twilight Princess port built on the Aurora framework — Vulkan-only, so most handhelds can't run it. Plus a side-detour into an upstream Dawn copy-paste bug, and a heads-up on Courage Reborn for non-Vulkan devices.

posts.title: Automating open-source port builds
posts.excerpt: Reproducible builds were the primary motivation: every port's binary is pinned to a specific upstream commit via .upstream-sha, every recipe is public, and anyone can re-run the workflow and diff the result. Six ports and one runtime rebuild themselves via a daily cron as the labor-saving bonus.
